notes: The optimized 1x4 multichip decoder supports 12352 logical tokens at batch 32. TP=4 shards every projection and both KV caches; packed gate/up preserves the separate projections' total weight bytes, the sharded decoder boundary removes an output restore without adding DRAM, and explicit prefill ownership plus row-parallel chunk reduction bound the live set. Every logical sequence length from 1 through 12352 is supported; this is not an aligned-only public contract. Paged-cache allocation rounds the next logical length, 12353, to 12416 physical tokens and reaches a hard device-DRAM allocation failure. The remaining reduction from the HF-advertised 40960 context is a freshly remeasured physical capacity limit.

capacity_evidence.hardware: four Blackhole p300c devices, 1x4 FABRIC_1D_RING, eight DRAM banks per device
capacity_evidence.workload: shape- and byte-faithful reservation for all 64 TP=4 decoder layers: two runtime layouts of every local BFP4 projection, local two-head BFP8 K/V caches, per-layer BF16 RoPE/norm state, persistent decode CCL buffers, and the ownership-aware peak BF16 prefill live set

advertised_context_impossibility.note: At batch 32 and sequence 40960, local TP-sharded KV alone is 45.634 GB/device and exceeds physical p300c DRAM before weights, RoPE, trace reservation, activations, CCL buffers, or allocator constraints.

decode_trace_state.components: two 32x128 BF16 RoPE tiles (8192 bytes each), 32 uint32 RoPE lookup indices (128 bytes), and 32 int32 cache-update indices (128 bytes)
decode_trace_state.lifecycle: fixed-address index buffers refreshed by host-to-device writes, then embedding lands in preallocated RoPE outputs before replay; no device allocation and no per-position retention while a trace is live
decode_trace_state.stack_note: The position inputs are model-independent and may be shared by a future layer stack; even if separately owned by all 64 layers, payload is bounded at 1064960 bytes.

rope_lookup_tables.note: Cosine and sine are each reserved in TILE and ROW_MAJOR layouts at 12352x128 BF16 so both prefill and allocation-free traced position refresh are modeled. The full-stack reservation conservatively includes 64 separate copies even though they are model-independent and shareable.

source_precision_policy.qkv_o_gate_up_down_weights: BFLOAT8_B after load-time fusion or transposition

translation.functional_precision_policy: All weights and activations use BFLOAT16 for the correctness-first stage; source BFLOAT8_B weight casts are intentionally not retained.

optimized_precision_policy.decode_residual: L1 width-sharded across 40 cores; down projection uses 32 cores

multichip_precision_policy.mesh: 1x4 FABRIC_1D_RING, TP=4 on mesh axis 1, two links
multichip_precision_policy.local_attention: 16 Q heads and 2 KV heads/device; 2048 attention channels/device
multichip_precision_policy.projection_weights: BFLOAT4_B with LoFi; QKV and gate/up are rank-packed, with one packed gate/up matmul replacing the repeated same-input pair; interleaved prefill and DRAM-sharded decode layouts
multichip_precision_policy.kv_cache: BFLOAT8_B, 2 local KV heads/device for contiguous and paged cache
multichip_precision_policy.collectives: BFLOAT16 Ring; two all-gathers and two reduce-scatters/layer; persistent decode buffers
multichip_precision_policy.decode_residual: The public/inter-layer boundary remains L1 width-sharded at 1280 channels/device across 20 cores with no gather, restore, reshard, or all-reduce between layers; QKV/down use 20 cores, packed gate/up uses 10 cores with in0_block_w=8, and O uses 8 cores
multichip_precision_policy.prefill_memory: projection chunks are at most 640 rows; row-parallel O/down chunks reduce-scatter before concatenation; completed intermediates are explicitly released
